2606.23382.title: Energy-Based Transformers as Predictors of Reading Difficulty
2606.23382.paper_summary: Transformer language models have become established tools for modeling human sentence processing, with measures such as surprisal and attention entropy serving as effective predictors of reading difficulty that together capture complementary aspects of processing load. Here, we explore a related class of transformer models: energy-based transformers, which provide a principled formal link to associative memory models, bringing processing research into direct contact with the broader literature on Hopfield networks and dense associative memory. To our knowledge, this is the first exploration of an energy-based transformer measure in computational psycholinguistics. Across reading-time corpora (Natural Stories, UCL eye-tracking, UCL self-paced reading), the energy measure is a robust predictor of reading times, providing significant fit beyond surprisal in all three. In a controlled experiment on relative clause processing, energy at a single layer captures the well-known object/subject asymmetry. We find evidence that it subsumes effects attributable to both attention entropy and surprisal, suggesting that energy may serve as a single unified predictor where multiple complementary measures have previously been required.
2606.23382.paper_summary_zh: Transformer語言模型已成為建模人類句子處理的成熟工具，驚訝度和注意力熵等指標作為閱讀難度的有效預測因子，捕捉了處理負荷的互補方面。在這裡，我們探討一類相關的Transformer模型：基於能量的Transformer，這提供了一個與聯想記憶模型的原則性形式聯繫，將處理研究與霍普菲爾德網絡和密集聯想記憶的更廣泛文獻直接聯繫起來。據我們所知，這是首次在計算心理語言學中探索基於能量的Transformer指標。在閱讀時間語料庫（自然故事、UCL眼動追蹤、UCL自我節奏閱讀）中，能量指標是閱讀時間的穩健預測因子，在所有三個語料庫中提供了超越驚訝度的顯著擬合。在一項關於關係子句處理的控制實驗中，單層的能量捕捉了眾所周知的物體/主體不對稱。我們發現證據表明，它包含了可歸因於注意力熵和驚訝度的效應，這表明能量可能作為一個統一的預測因子，而之前需要多個互補的指標。
2606.23382.author: Jakub Dotlacil et.al.
